blocks.text: watched her walk back toward the others. She didn't look over her shoulder. People who have nothing to hide usually do.
had read it as genuine grief. It would look exactly like this if it were.
had held both possibilities open. Both could be true at once.
kept his read to himself for now.

blocks.speaker: Julian Crane
blocks.content: Davenport asked me to stay available.

blocks.speaker: Mara Voss
blocks.content: That is not the same as spending four hours running your own investigation.

blocks.speaker: Julian Crane
blocks.content: Davenport is going to file it as suspicious death. Tox is seventy-two hours minimum. In that window the group disperses, memories shift, people align their stories.

blocks.speaker: Julian Crane
blocks.content: He doesn't know what I know.

blocks.speaker: Mara Voss
blocks.content: Julian. You don't know enough yet either.

blocks.speaker: Julian Crane
blocks.content: I know someone placed that water glass. I know someone went through that window at 9:51. I know one person in that group had a reason to argue with Marcus that nobody else knew about. And I know Wren Cole saw something she hasn't told anyone yet.

blocks.speaker: Julian Crane
blocks.content: Because she told me she needed to think.

blocks.speaker: Julian Crane
blocks.content: I'll be back in an hour.

blocks.prompt: Will Julian be back in an hour?

options.label: Yes. He said it and he means it.
No. And Mara already knows that.
